title: The Twenty Doctors and Wapol
description: On pages 11–13, the twenty doctors and wapol as dalton shelters the crew and explains Drum Island’s fall under Wapol while the search for the island’s last doctor continues.
scene: The Twenty Doctors and Wapol. Dalton shelters the crew and explains Drum Island’s fall under Wapol while the search for the island’s last doctor continues. The focus is the story beat, character decisions, and dialogue driving this part.

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: of all the places she could be, she just HAS to be there...

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Well, we better go call her right away then! This is an emergency.

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: As much as I'd like to, there is no way to contact her.

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: What!?

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: And she calls herself a doctor!? What's with this woman!?

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: her skills as a doctor are undeniable,

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: But she's bit of a kook... and almost 140 years old at that, too.

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: 140!? And she's still alive!?

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: other than that... Ah, that's right. She happens to like pickled plums.

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Then what do the people of this country do when they're sick or injured!?

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Well, she comes down from the mountain whenever she feels like it,

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Finds patients to treat, and afterwards...

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: She takes anything she wants from their homes as payment.

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: That is one mean old Hag.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: She's basically a pirate!

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: How does this old Granny even get down from the mountains...?

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: According to certain eyewitness,

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: She supposedly rides down on a sleigh on Mootlit nights...

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Heeeeeee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee he

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: That is why the people call her a witch...

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Others have reportedly seen a most bizarre creature travelling along with her...

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: GAHHA! I knew it! I warned you guys! I told you guys about the snow monsters!

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: I just knew there had to be witches and yetis lurking among the snowy mountain peaks!

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Ahhhh! Please, God! Don't let them find me!

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Yes, although she's this country's only doctor,

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: She's also someone who I'd prefer to have little to do with...

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: In any case, I'm afraid the only thing you can do is wait until she comes down...

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: That can't be...

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: What in the world...

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: How the hell is it possible for there to be only one doctor in an entire country!?

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Hey, Nami!

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Nami!

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Slap!

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Can you hear me?

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: And just what the hell do you think you're doing!?

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: MANN...

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Ah, you're up!

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Listen, we can't get a doctor unless we go up a mountain,

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: So we'll be hiking up.

summary: This part centers on the twenty doctors and wapol, clarifying the immediate conflict and how it moves the chapter toward its next major turn.
